title: KPop Demon Hunters Wins Golden Globe for Best Motion Picture - Animated
text: Netflix's animated musical adventure KPop Demon Hunters claimed the Golden Globe for Best Motion Picture in the animation category, triumphing over competitors including Zootopia 2, Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba - The Movie: Infinity Castle, and others. The Sony Pictures Animation production, directed by Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans, follows HUNTR/X—a trio of idols who moonlight as demon hunters. The group consists of characters Rumi, Mira, and Zoey, voiced by Arden Cho, May Hong, and Ji-young Yoo respectively, with singing performed by artists Ejae, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami. Released in June 2025, the film became Netflix's most-watched film of all time with over 325 million viewers. Its soundtrack achieved unprecedented chart performance, with four songs reaching the Billboard Hot 100—a first for any soundtrack. The victory follows the film's earlier Critics' Choice Award win, positioning it as the clear favorite for an Academy Award nomination. The recognition underscores the project's cultural impact since its theatrical debut. A sequel is scheduled for 2029 release, while the franchise has expanded through partnerships including a Fortnite collaboration and a Dungeons & Dragons game book kickstarter.
